item.text: {"summary":"Live-search-informed initial feature hypothesis focused on a physical redshift-to-distance representation not yet directly covered by prior photometric color, flux, sky, clustering, or SDSS-rule feature families.","hypotheses":[{"title":"Cosmological Absolute-Magnitude Proxies","summary":"Transform observed ugriz magnitudes and spectroscopic redshift into approximate luminosity-distance and absolute-magnitude features to separate nearby stars from extragalactic galaxies and QSOs.","feature_family":"cosmological_distance_magnitude_features","feature_strategy":"Keep raw ugriz magnitudes, redshift, alpha/delta, and one-hot spectral_type/galaxy_population, then add target-free cosmology-derived features. Clip redshift only for numerical distance calculations, preserve flags for redshift<=0, near_zero_redshift, and high_redshift regimes. Using a fixed simple flat LCDM approximation such as H0=70 and Omega_m=0.3, compute luminosity_distance_proxy, log_luminosity_distance, distance_modulus, and per-band approximate absolute magnitudes M_u through M_z as apparent_magnitude - distance_modulus. Add absolute-magnitude summaries across bands, color-vs-absolute-brightness interactions such as M_r with g-r and u-g, and simple redshift-regime interactions with spectral_type and galaxy_population. Do not fit any intermediate supervised model or use target-derived encodings.","baseline_model_panel":"Use the same simple balanced multiclass panel for measurement: regularized multinomial logistic regression on standardized numeric/one-hot features, a class-weighted random-forest or extra-trees model, and one modest class-balanced gradient-boosted tree model such as LightGBM with class_weight='balanced' or CatBoost with auto_class_weights='Balanced'.","model_panel_rationale":"The linear model tests whether the physical distance transform creates nearly separable axes, while the tree models test threshold and interaction effects between absolute brightness, color, redshift, and metadata without making the hypothesis depend on model tuning.","validation_strategy":"Use 5-fold StratifiedKFold with balanced accuracy, preserving identical folds across baseline comparisons; apply class balancing inside each fold and report per-class recall to verify that STAR/QSO gains are not hiding GALAXY losses.","materialization_hint":"Implement as a feature block after loading train/test together for deterministic transforms only. Build a small vectorized luminosity-distance approximation or precomputed interpolation grid over observed redshift values, protect log calculations with redshift floors, add invalid-redshift flags before clipping, then append the derived distance-modulus and absolute-magnitude columns to the standard raw/one-hot baseline feature matrix.","expected_signal":"If useful, CV should show improved balanced accuracy mainly through better STAR versus extragalactic separation at near-zero redshift and better QSO/GALAXY separation among high-redshift objects; tree feature importances should rank distance_modulus, M_r/M_i, and redshift-regime interactions near the top.","novelty_confidence":"medium","risk":"This overlaps partially with prior redshift-scaled luminosity proxies, but differs by using an explicit distance-modulus/absolute-magnitude physical representation. Risk is highest for stars or synthetic rows with nonphysical redshift values, so clipped-distance features must be paired with raw redshift and invalid/near-zero flags to avoid numerical artifacts.","sources":["https://arxiv.org/abs/1909.10963","https://arxiv.org/abs/1603.09708","https://arxiv.org/abs/1410.8139","https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Distance_modulus","https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luminosity_distance"]}]}
